title: 👑 Elite challenge ⭐⭐⭐⭐: the final gate of the journey

questions.prompt: Emma is lost in the souk and needs help. Which question does SHE ask?
questions.explanation: When YOU need help, you ASK FOR it: "Can you help me, please?" ✓. The common trap is "Can I help you?", which you say to OFFER help to someone else. "You can help" is not a question, and "How much is it?" asks a price.

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ bread do you want for the picnic?"
questions.explanation: "Bread" is uncountable — we say "a loaf of bread", never "two breads" — so we ask with "How much": How much bread do you want? ✓ The common trap is "How many" because bread feels like a thing you can count, but it is uncountable.

questions.prompt: Which sentence compares two things correctly?

options.text: The library is like a treasure room.
The library is as a treasure room.
The library likes a treasure room.
The library is liking a treasure room.

questions.explanation: The comparison pattern is "noun + be + like + noun": the library is like a treasure room ✓ (like = similar to). "Is as" is the common trap and is wrong here. "Likes" and "is liking" use the verb "to like" (to enjoy), which changes the meaning.

questions.prompt: Three of these -ed verbs end in the sound /t/. Which one is DIFFERENT?
questions.explanation: "Played" ends in a vowel sound, so its -ed is pronounced /d/ — it is the odd one out ✓. The other three follow a voiceless sound (sh, ch, p), so their -ed is /t/: washed, watched, helped all end in /t/.

questions.prompt: Read: "(1) Emma wants to buy a small carpet. (2) 'How many is this carpet?' she asks. (3) The shopkeeper says it is twenty dinars. (4) Emma cannot carry it alone." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: This question asks you to spot the faulty sentence. "How many is this carpet?" is wrong: a price always takes "how much", so it must be "How much is this carpet?" ✓. Sentences 1, 3 and 4 correctly use "wants to buy", the answer, and "cannot carry".

questions.prompt: Only ONE of these sentences is completely correct. Which one?

options.text: The globe is behind of the door.
I want that you visit the lab.
You can put the map between the two windows.
She can to speak English fluently.

questions.explanation: Sentence c is correct: "can" + base verb "put", and "between" with two things ✓. "Behind of" (a) is wrong — "behind" takes no "of". "I want that you visit" (b) is a translation trap; English uses "I want you to visit". "Can to speak" (d) wrongly adds "to" after "can".
